rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC DBA GREAT WESTERN VS. MR.PICKWICK'S BRITISH PUB & RESTAURANT LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 26, 2025 Line 1. ASSIGNEE GARY E. LOONEY, DBA COLLECTRONICS OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Vacate Judgment. Assignee Gary E. Looney's unopposed Motion To Vacate Judgment is DENIED. Assignee appears to move under CCP section 473(d). The section provides as follows: "The court may, upon motion of the injured party or its own motion, correct clerical mistakes in its judgment or orders entered so as to conform the judgment or order directed, and may, on motion of either party or notice to the other party, set aside any void judgment or order." Assignee seeks an order vacating a September 19, 2024, renewal of judgment, the original judgment having been entered on August 14, 2007. Assignee, however, has not demonstrated that the September 19, 2024, renewal of judgment is "void." Assignee has not demonstrated that a renewal of judgment is void simply because a judgment debtor filed for bankruptcy protection at some unspecified time before the renewal was entered. In any event, the record on this motion is devoid of any evidence of any bankruptcy filing, let alone one that may render the September 19, 2024, renewal of judgment void. Separately, the court is not convinced that the instant motion was properly served. Assignee served the motion on counsel Gerald L. White. Mr. White has not made an appearance for any judgment debtor, including Mr. Pickwick's British Pub & Restaurant, Ltd. or Sandie R. Price. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BLAISDELL ET AL VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Propounded On Defendant City Of San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 26, 2025 Line 4. 1 - PLAINTIFFs ROBERT BLAISDELL, and PORTALES, DAVID BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Propounded On Defendant City Of San Francisco. Continued to April 8, 2025. Each party is ordered to provide a courtesy copy of each document it filed that it wants considered in connection with this motion no later than March 28, 2025. Each filed document must be fastened individually, conforming to how each document appears on the register of actions. This requirement seems self-evident, yet it has not been followed in this case (and others). Repeated violations will be addressed with sanctions. The court notes that any party can obviate the need for any further continuance of this motion for lack of courtesy copies by simply lodging a complete set of all documents related to the motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 26, 2025 Line 6. PLAINTIFF ELISA ROSSI Renewed Motion To Seal Portions Of Plaintiff complaint. Plaintiff Elisa Rossi's unopposed Renewed Motion of Plaintiff Elisa Rossi to Seal Portions of the Complaint is GRANTED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
